criteria.label: Define eps, min_samples, and the core / border / noise point types.
criteria.example: Write: core if |N_eps(p)| ≥ min_samples; border if within eps of a core but not core; noise otherwise (label −1).

criteria.label: Explain how DBSCAN grows a cluster from a core point.
criteria.example: Describe density reachability: start at unvisited core, add all points reachable through a chain of cores each within eps of the previous.

criteria.label: Contrast DBSCAN with k-means on cluster count, shape, and noise handling.
criteria.example: Table: DBSCAN — emergent k, arbitrary shapes, noise −1; k-means — fixed k, spherical blobs, all points assigned.

criteria.label: Run DBSCAN on scaled data and report cluster count and noise fraction.
criteria.example: Pipeline(StandardScaler, DBSCAN(eps=0.5, min_samples=5)).fit_predict(X); print n_clusters and (labels==-1).mean().

criteria.label: Use a k-distance plot to propose an initial eps and justify min_samples.
criteria.example: Plot sorted 4-th NN distances with min_samples=5 on scaled 8-D data; choose eps at elbow 0.42; cite min_samples ≥ d+1 rule.

criteria.label: Identify when DBSCAN is inappropriate and name a better alternative.
criteria.example: Scenario: one tight urban cluster and one sparse rural ring at different densities — single eps fails; suggest HDBSCAN or separate regional models.
